findings.evidence: `nl -ba ...Relative.lean` shows `@[expose]` at lines 50 and 67; the file already has `fixedOnSubgroup_def`, `boundaryFixingSubgroup_def`, and `@[simp]` membership iff lemmas, so unfolding the bodies is not needed.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from both definitions and provide the needed public lemmas instead of requiring unfolding.
findings.issue: `fixedOnSubgroup` and `boundaryFixingSubgroup` are marked `@[expose]`, exposing implementation bodies even though users only need the characteristic lemmas and specialization lemmas.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "fixedOnSubgroup_(empty|union|iUnion|antitone)" code/TauCeti` returns no hits, while Mathlib has `fixingSubgroup_empty`, `fixingSubgroup_antitone`, `fixingSubgroup_union`, and `fixingSubgroup_iUnion` in `Mathlib/GroupTheory/GroupAction/FixingSubgroup.lean:140-160`.
findings.fix: Add wrapper lemmas such as `fixedOnSubgroup_empty`, `fixedOnSubgroup_union`, `fixedOnSubgroup_iUnion`, and `fixedOnSubgroup_antitone` (with appropriate simp attributes, e.g. empty) proved from Mathlib’s generic lemmas.
findings.issue: The public wrapper `fixedOnSubgroup` lacks the inherited set-operation/order API, so users cannot state the empty/union/iUnion/antitone behavior under the new name without unfolding to `fixingSubgroup`.

summary: The membership API is now simplified correctly, but the new public subgroup wrapper relies on exposed bodies instead of providing the wrapper-level fixing-subgroup API promised for downstream use.
